items.title: Nuclear Strike Required: Antarctic Co-op Horror Unfrost Revealed on Steam
items.summary: UNFROST is a co-op Antarctic horror FPS from Nord Unit where conventional weapons cannot kill the Riftborne — players must gather forensic evidence, configure a nuclear payload correctly, and extract before impact. The 1-4 player Steam title was revealed July 15, 2026 with no release date or price

items.title: Phasmophobia Quality of Life Preview #2
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Following on from our recent preview of the Quality of Life update launching on July 21st, we wanted to share some further details including the newer features - including another look at the reworked 13 Willow
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Following on from our recent preview of the Quality of Life update launching on July 21st, we wanted to share some further details including the newer features - including another look at the reworked 13 Willow Street.

The New

As part of the Quality of Life update, we’re introducing a handful of new gameplay features that aim to improve the overall experience of playing Phasmophobia. 

Read the below for some details on these additions.

  • Adding keybinds for Equipment slots. PC players will be able to switch Equipment with the 1, 2, 3 and 4 keys. These keys can be rebound.

    [/*]

  • This addition will only be available for PC players.

    [/*]

  • Adding a keybind to be able to inspect the Watch at any time.

    [/*]

  • This will be defaulted to Left DPAD on a controller and H on keyboard. Players can rebind this via Options > Controls.

    [/*]

  • A separate Photo can now be taken capturing an EMF Level 5 reading.

    [/*]

  • Photo Cameras will now feature the same highlight frame as Video Cameras. 

    [/*]

  • On screen indicator only on Tier 2 and 3 Photo Cameras, while Tier 1 will have the light change colour.

    [/*]

  • When capturing Media, if multiple are present, the evidence that provides the biggest reward or is Unique will be prioritised.

    [/*]

  • New Ghost: the ██████████

    [/*]

  • Players will have a higher chance of getting this Ghost in their contracts for the first few weeks following release.

    [/*]

  • Added new walking animations and turning animations to fix several issues:

    [/*]

  • Character legs will no longer use procedural IK and will instead use animations.

    • The character body will no longer animate with the walking animations which caused held items to move while walking.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Improvements to the overall VR character:

    [/*]

  • New character animations.

    • Improvements to the belt to improve usability.

      [/*]

    • The character will now turn instantly depending on where you are looking instead of at set angles.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • The next quality of life update will bring a completely new VR character rig with further improvements:

    [/*]

  • Further improvements to the belt's usability.

    • Improvements to crouching.

      [/*]

    • Improved character animations.

      [/*]

    • Held items will no longer sway when walking.

      [/*]

    • The character's position will accurately represent your real life body position.

      [/*]
    [/*]

13 Willow Street

As with all our reworked maps, 13 Willow Street will retain its overall original layout with a few minor changes. The core of these reworks has always been bringing the visuals up to our standards while letting each location speak for itself. As much as we love the original versions of these maps, giving them their own identities and stories has been something we’ve always wanted to achieve and we’re excited that we’ve been able to. 

Following 6 Tanglewood Drive, we know that 13 Willow Street holds a special place in a Ghost Hunter’s heart. We’ve kept that at the forefront while developing the rework, making sure that those first few steps inside feel new but familiar, and entice you to explore during each and every contract.

Check out the screenshots below for a look inside 13 Willow Street.

13 Willow Street will launch alongside the Quality of Life update on July 21st, 2026 for all platforms. Stay tuned to our social channels and our official Discord server for exact release timings. As is tradition with a new map, a new ID Card and Badge will be available upon completion of the map’s challenge. By correctly identifying 50 ghosts (and surviving) Ghost Hunters will earn the Sun Seeker set, as seen below.

We’ll also be running a new Twitch Drops campaign for the first week after launch. Players will be able to earn the animated and static versions of the Cool Cat ID Card and Badge between 21st July - 28th July by watching a total of 2 hours of Phasmophobia streams.

The animated version will be exclusively available via Kinetic Games Partner channels.

We’re looking forward to getting this update out to you next week and will be keeping an eye on every bit of feedback sent to us on social, in Discord or otherwise.

items.title: 5 Video Games Like Phasmophobia
items.summary: "Phasmophobia" has you and your friends locked in terror until all the horrors are conquered. Need more like it? These five games creep close to the goal.
items.content:
Kinetic Games Among the scariest games released within the past several years is "Phasmophobia," developed by the British studio Kinetic Games. The game is available to play solo or online cooperatively, with players controlling a team of ghost hunters. Each level has players investigate a haunted site to determine what kind of spirit is prowling the area. The game ended up getting fixed because it had too many scares as a testament to its overall fear factor.

The surprise success of "Phasmophobia" led to many team-based ghost-hunting games filling the market, several of which are quite good. These games each feature their own memorably haunted sites and unique ghosts to keep players on their toes. With that particular niche in mind, we're listing games that place players in the midst of the haunting as they try to determine what spectral activity they've found themselves up against. Here are five video games like "Phasmophobia," all picked to help keep the paranormal frights coming after you in the dark.

LostOneTeam This game is technically still within its early access phase, but worth checking out: the 2022 title "Ghost Exile" by LostOneTeam. It has teams of up to four players working together to examine sites with suspected hauntings in desperate need of an exorcism. Like "Phasmophobia" and many other games on this list, players use environmental clues to determine what kind of phantasm they're facing. Using their equipment, the players then perform an exorcism, though there are lethally dire consequences if they misidentify the apparitions.

"Ghost Exile" brings the spectral terror and crime scene investigation elements to the forefront of its gameplay, but with its own twists. The game offers more customization options for players to make the experience feel truly their own, right down to character design. Like "Phasmophobia," the game also possesses a voice recognition mechanic, allowing players to communicate with ghosts during their examination.

With ghosts that can level up and an unlockable skill tree, "Ghost Exile" brings a surprising amount of depth to its familiar premise. On Reddit , a fan enthusiastically shared their love of the game, writing "Dude, get Ghost Exile. Not only is it scarier than Phasmophobia, it has a ton more to it as far as gameplay and they are actively updating the game all the time. I cannot overstate how much I love that game."

Clock Wizard Games The 2023 game "Demonologist" dials up the sheer terror and supernatural menace compared to many of the other titles on this list. The game again has players either work alone or with teams of up to four to investigate paranormal locations. After identifying the types of apparitions that present around a given site, players can then work together to exorcise the lethal entities; familiar, but it works. The ghosts feel much more dangerous than they do in "Phasmophobia" as they will lash out and stalk players investigating them.

"Demonologist" is definitely one of those horror games that you should play during the spooky season . This is a game that heavily evokes a pervasively unsettling atmosphere and ramps up the suspense as the various spirits go on the offensive. To elevate the scares, the game features incredibly detailed environments and an immersive sound design, really putting players on edge. As Redditor u/DonkeyShaunJr put it, "The level of detail of the hidden items, the lore, it really helped us keep engaged with the game."

An even more intense take on a familiar premise, "Demonologist" is best played with friends, rather than taking on these hellish frights alone.

Dreambyte Games The 2024 game "Forewarned" has more of a grave robbing premise rather than one revolving around paranormal investigations. The game has teams of up to four players raid ancient Egyptian tombs for treasure, only to incur supernatural wrath at each burial site. The investigative elements that are present in the gameplay are used to identify the types of undead monsters prowling the sites. With this knowledge, players can exploit the mummies' strengths and weaknesses as they recover holy relics and escape in one piece.

With a cultural focus on Egyptian legends and some slightly silly stereotypes, "Forewarned" certainly stands out from other supernatural investigation games out on the market. The game's tomb raiding premise also helps differentiate it, while providing players with an alternative objective to other games on this list. A fan on Reddit described it as being "like Phas with mummies and randomly generated maps. Its awesome."

Like "Phasmophobia," the game also enjoys virtual reality headset functionality, making it a particularly terrifying game to play in VR . A team-based game that does something much more distinct with the usual premise, "Forewarned" is a lot of fun when coordinating with friends over the immersive device.

Renderise After initially launching in an early access phase on Steam in 2022, "Ghost Watchers" got its official release in 2025. Like every other game on this list, the title allows up to four players working together to battle ghosts. Players explore haunted locations like abandoned houses, police stations, and ghost towns to determine what kind of paranormal activity is afoot. From there, gamers have a whole arsenal of tools to help them fight back against the deadly enemies and end their threat for good.

With dozens of unique enemy types and an immersive variety of environments to explore and purge of phantasms, "Ghost Watchers" reflects its years of hard work from the developers. Renderise has crafted a genuinely spooky experience that, like all the other games on this list, is best experienced in the company of friends. Because many of the enemies in this game are truly terrifying, purging them is all the more satisfying. A moody little ghost hunt, "Ghost Watchers" is the most recent title on this list and one well worth checking out. As Steam reviewer Skyblade799 put it, "Of all of the ghost-identification games ... this actually might be my favorite."

Hitchcock Games Another terrifyingly fun twist on these familiar ghost-hunting genre tropes is the 2019 game "Pacify." Developed by Shawn Hitchcock and published by Hitchcock Games, the game has teams of up to four players investigating haunted sites featuring various apparitions, including one of a young girl. Players must avoid provoking the direct wrath of these supernatural entities and figure out how to pacify these paranormal threats long enough to escape. In addition to single-player and co-op modes, the game also has a PvP mode that pits the players against each other in an office setting.

items.title: Phasmophobia Quality of Life Update Preview
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we’re going to give you more detail on the upcoming Quality of Life update launching alongside the 13 Willow Street rework on July 21st, 2026. As mentioned in our recent blogpost , the Quality of Life upda
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we’re going to give you more detail on the upcoming Quality of Life update launching alongside the 13 Willow Street rework on July 21st, 2026.

As mentioned in our recent blogpost, the Quality of Life update itself has been split into two due to the sheer number of fixes & improvements we’ll be implementing. This first update is the bigger of the two, but you can expect the second part in late August.

The below list focuses on changes and fixes coming with this month’s update while the next update will contain further improvements.

While there’s a lot in the below list, it’s not everything that’ll be launching for Phasmophobia on July 21st, with the update bringing with it new keybinds, including PC players being able to use 1, 2, 3 and 4 for changing inventory slots, a couple of improvements for evidence collection and of course, the reworked 13 Willow Street. We’ll detail all of these in a blog the week before launch.

Changes

  • The character hand animations for grabbing, dropping, moving doors and hovering over interactable or grabbable objects have been removed to speed up gameplay.

    [/*]

  • The item drop animation will still play for others in the session, but will not be visible by the Player.

    [/*]

  • Cosmetics that are no longer able to be earned will not be visible.

    [/*]

  • The camera zoom in the Customisation shop will now focus on the selected part of the character.

    [/*]

  • On Sunny Meadows, the Ghost will now only lock the doors to a wing if both it and a player are inside that wing.

    [/*]

  • Eye Adaptation will now adjust the lighting quicker when opening and closing the Journal.

    [/*]

  • Console players will now have the ability to enable/disable Eye Adaptation.

    [/*]

  • The Obake’s shapeshift will now count towards the Ghost video objective.

    [/*]

  • You can now capture any Media for the Apocalypse challenge, instead of only a photo.

    [/*]

  • Recording the Ghost Orbs in the Tutorial is now required, instead of only getting it inside the video camera’s screen.

    [/*]

  • Improved the lighting around the player, allowing for better visibility in darker areas.

    [/*]

  • Added an icon to the Player ID Card to show if that player is speaking.

    [/*]

  • Adjusted the visuals on the PSVR2 player list popup

    [/*]

  • Event board map leaflets are now spread over multiple pages

    [/*]

  • Players can now cancel Use animations by pressing the Use button during the animation, allowing for equipment to be quickly toggled without waiting for the animation to finish

    [/*]

  • The Journal has now been moved closer for easier readability.

    [/*]

  • The Journal animation is now significantly faster.

    [/*]

  • Character legs will now use animations, instead of procedurally generated animations when moving

    [/*]

  • Improved the detection of photo evidence.

    [/*]

  • Higher priority unique photos will always take priority.

    • Unique photos will always take priority over duplicates.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • VR - The character will now rotate instantly to match the head rotation when looking straight ahead. When looking down, the character rotation will only turn when an angle threshold has been met to allow easier use of the belt.

    [/*]

  • VR - The Journal will now be instantly opened upon use.

    [/*]

General Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where players could be seen through the walls of the Truck when viewing the CCTV.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Voice Recognition to not work if switching from ‘Voice’ to ‘System’.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the cursor would not appear after completing a contract when using a controller.

    [/*]

  • Improved network stability to reduce disconnects.

    [/*]

Player Character Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where binding Crouch to X (PlayStation controllers) or A (Xbox controller) prevented players from using the Journal.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused dead player’s eyes to appear incorrect.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the ID Card to be displayed in front of the character model in the Customisation shop.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to become stuck and unable to continue playing after being revived.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused cosmetic settings to be reset if a Player joins or leaves a lobby.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in Nell’s Diner that caused the player to become stuck in the corner of the Freezer Room.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed infinite sprinting while closing the Journal.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused issues with the character model’s right arm when holding certain pieces of Equipment.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the cursor to become invisible in the Customisation shop if the left and right mouse buttons were clicked alternatively.

    [/*]

  • Fixed various visual inconsistencies and errors with character models, cosmetics, equipment and more.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the audio from the Customisation shop’s UI to be louder than intended.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a player’s Journal to visually jitter when they swap Equipment.

    [/*]

Equipment Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Flashlight to turn invisible when picking up another item.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the light from a Flashlight to be visible after death.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Equipment to become invisible.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 1 and 3 Firelights to be placed unlit if placed during the lighting animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Equipment to toggle off if dropped while holding the Use button.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Equipment sounds to become muffled for player’s picking them up after another player dropped them.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Flashlight to be unusable if picked up during a placement animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented players from placing the Flashlight back in the Truck.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the screen to become blurry after enabling Night Vision when using the CCTV unit in the Truck.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 3 Incense to not animate correctly when activated.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a second Salt to be placed if the Player opened the Journal during the placement animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Sound Recorder visuals and audio to play when not in the player’s hand.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Spirit Box to become unusable if the player switched to text recognition after picking the Spirit Box up.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 1 Head Gear to be tilted when in use.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Flashlight to appear in-hand when equipping 2 handed Cursed Possessions such as the Haunted Mirror.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that could lock the player if they dropped the Photo Camera during it’s placement animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused certain Equipment to be Used after switching to a different piece of Equipment.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused text prompts for the Ouija Board to not work if the player switched from Voice Recognition while using the board.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented a moving Ouija Board to not be recordable by Video Cameras.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented a burning Crucifix to not be recordable by the Sound Recorder.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a second Salt to be placed if the Player opened the Journal during the placement animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused inconsistencies with Incense used/unused visuals.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that could cause Cursed Possessions to become invisible if dropped during a pick-up animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Haunted Mirror to be blocked by other objects when in Use.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed players to light the Summoning Circle when at 0 Sanity.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the player to be softlocked if using a Crucifix at the start of a Hunt.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the player to cancel the Ouija Board answer by either dropping or picking up the board if using text recognition.

    [/*]

Ghost Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused all Ghost types to be able to mimic the Yurei’s door ability.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the Ghost from interacting with doors it had already interacted with during a Ghost Event.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Kormos to be able to hear players during the grace period after a Hunt.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to hear players during a Hunt, despite the player having used Incense.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Kormos to go to the incorrect floor after detecting a player.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to kill players through walls or from a greater distance than intended.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Phantom to perform its ability on Dead players or players outside.

    [/*]

Location Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused the setup timer to not count down if playing on Amateur/Intermediate on Brownstone High School.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on Bleasdale Farmhouse where the door to the hiding spot in the Foyer could be opened even if the hiding spot was blocked.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on Brownstone High School where the side gate could be opened without the key from the Truck.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the washing machines in Sunny Meadows to not be able to be turned on.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the player to be unable to open the gates on Camp Woodwind and Maple Lodge Campsite despite the cursor prompt being visible.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several issues in the Tutorial that caused the player to be unable to exit the Truck or interact with any items.

    [/*]

VR Fixes

With this first Quality of Life update, we’re addressing some immediate issues that VR players are encountering. In our August update, we’ll be implementing a brand new character rig for VR allowing for improved gameplay and animations. We’ll share more details on this in a future blog.

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused visual jitters for the Video Camera when attached to as Tier 2 or 3 Tripod.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused Stamina to not recover while crouched.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused Equipment to disappear if the player walks in to a waist-high object.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that allowed players to grab Head Gear off of other player’s heads.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused player height inconsistencies in Multiplayer.

    [/*]

  • VR - Implemented a left hand pose when holding the Ouija Board.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that prevented the Tier 3 Igniter Use animation from playing correctly.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused the character model’s head to become detached from their body when walking sideways.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused the Head Gear to drop to the floor when placed back on the Truck rack.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that allowed players to skip parts of the Tutorial by using teleport movement.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that prevented players from recording evidence whilst holding the Journal.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused the VR UI ray to be affected by the hand animations.

    [/*]

  • VR - Fixed an issue that caused the interact animation to player when looking at another Player’s items.

    [/*]

items.title: New Co-Op Horror Game on Steam is Phasmophobia Meets Lethal Company
Phasmophobia’s 2026 roadmap is now all about the big fix
items.summary: Phasmophobia’s 2026 roadmap shifts from 1.0 hype to fixes, polish, map reworks, and a stronger 2027 launch.
items.content:
Phasmophobia was supposed to spend 2026 sprinting toward its big 1.0 moment, but Kinetic Games has hit the brakes and changed the plan. Instead of rushing the full release out the door, 2026 is now looking like a repair, polish, and trust-rebuilding year before Phasmophobia finally reaches 1.0 in the second half of 2027. Honestly, for a game built around ghosts ruining your evening, that might be the smarter move.

The new roadmap puts the focus on fixing the experience players already have. First up is the 13 Willow Street rework, arriving on July 21, 2026, alongside a Quality of Life update. The layout is staying familiar, so veteran ghost hunters will not suddenly get lost in the walls, but the house is being refreshed with redesigned rooms, more atmosphere, extra objects, and more environmental storytelling. Basically, same haunted home, new nightmare smell.

A second Quality of Life update is planned for August, followed by a Unity 6 upgrade in October. That one is a big deal because Kinetic says it should improve stability, lighting, accessibility options like color blind preferences, and general performance. After that, November brings the second Player Character update, adding more customization, cosmetics, better animations, a cleaner Shop UI, and improved character models.

The bigger stuff is still coming, just later. The 1.0 launch has moved to 2027, along with the next new map and the Nintendo Switch 2 version. Kinetic has also shown plans for improved ghost models, including Patient 07, and a new Conduits system that will replace the Bone collectible and add more lore to investigations. On top of that, 2026 still has returning seasonal events, the 42 Edgefield Road rework, Weekly Challenge changes, and new Voice Recognition features on the way.

It is a bit like what Demonologist tries to do in the same ghost-hunting space: scary contracts, spooky locations, and plenty of panic. But Phasmophobia still has that special “everyone screams over the radio and dies in the van doorway” energy that keeps players coming back.

So yes, 2026 is not the grand finale anymore. It is the cleanup run before the big hunt. If you are ready to grab your EMF reader, annoy a ghost, and pretend you are brave, use our price comparison tool to find the best prices for Phasmophobia .

Passionate gamer whose first memory is playing games like Doom and Warcraft, turned into a professional World of Warcraft streamer, and now passionate about everything games-related.

items.title: PHASMOPHOBIA Map Rework Coming This Summer With 1.0 Launching Next Year
items.summary: PHASMOPHOBIA is coming, loaded with redesigned ghosts, reworked maps, and more! Art director Corey Dixon breaks down how they're preparing for version 1.0 and what we have to look forward to.
items.content:
Add Us See more FANGORIA stories when you search on Google. Share Facebook Reddit Bluesky X Email Copy URL Phasmophobia is one of the biggest success stories in multiplayer horror games. To this day, it's still wildly popular, with players finding the investigative and paranormal gameplay loop fun and engaging. Almost 6 years since its early access launch in 2020, it’s ready to launch into its 1.0 version in 2027. Once a solo dev effort by CEO Daniel Knight, he expanded Kinetic Studios to grow the game with future content and updates.

To save time, Phasmophobia was built with stock assets from the Unity Asset Store. But over the past several years, the development team has been continually replacing them with new original assets to give Phasmophobia its own visual identity before the 1.0 launch.

At Summer Game Fest 2026, we sat down and spoke with art director Corey Dixon about preparing for version 1.0 and how the game’s sudden success impacted development.

For more, check out the best new horror games we saw at Summer Game Fest .

The development team showed me a sneak peek of their upcoming rework of one of the maps, 13 Willow Street, which features a single one-story home for players to explore. The newly implemented art assets include adding more lore to the home, such as portraying the family as cat lovers. The home is littered with cat photos, some of which are the real life cats of some of the developers!

Dixon said that so far, the current map reworks, such as 6 Tanglewood Drive and both Bleasdale and Grafton Farmhouses, have been positively received by the Phasmophobia community. “Our equipment and map reworks have always been massively praised for the level of quality they bring to the game,” he explained.

“There’s usually a little bit of apprehension at first because the community is so used to playing within our legacy maps, but the overall consensus is that reworks breathe new life into the game, from adding new hiding spots to just making everything that little bit more immersive.”

Although not every single update was a hit. Last month, Phasmophobia launched the Player Character Update, which lets players customize their characters with options like clothing, hair color, and cosmetics. But it also brought along weird animation jank with limbs and objects overlapping. The team has since apologized for the less-than-stellar update and has vowed to continue fixing the game.

“Its launch showed us there’s still work to do in that area,” Dixon said. “Our goal will always be to deliver the highest-quality experience possible, and this time we didn’t hit that standard.”

The development team is also redesigning the ghosts to give them more distinct visual identities. Right now, they look more like zombies than your typical ghost. I was shown several of the new redesigns, including ghosts modeled after the kinds of deaths their human forms experienced. For example, a ghost that drowned would have wet hair, while a ghost that burned to death would be on fire. This would give each ghost its own unique backstory.

With each ghost design, the team thought they could further separate the visual design from the scares. The process involved picking themes and pushing them as far as possible without the designs looking too cliche or monster-like. “It allowed us to really push the theme for each one and ensure they had a lot of individuality,” Dixon said.

While Phasmophobia ’s previous 2026 roadmap included launching into 1.0, it’s been revised to 2027, as the team continues to focus on quality and speed. Other improvements include more immersive audio, such as spatial sound, environmental enhancements, and updated weather systems.

With the 1.0 version, Kinetic Games is also looking to launch the Nintendo Switch 2 version, which will bring even more new players into the fold. “Dan was a one-man band when he launched Phasmophobia , but its instant success changed that very quickly,” Dixon explained. “The sheer number of fans was beyond anything we expected, but it’s been a blessing. We can now develop our own ideas, listen to feedback, and strike a balance between what the community wants and our own creative vision.”

The 13 Willow Street map rework will launch for free on July 21, and Phasmophobia 1.0 is set to release in the second half of 2027.

items.title: Phasmophobia 2026 and Beyond
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Over the last month and a half, we’ve been reading the discussions about Phasmophobia across so many different channels and taking note of the biggest issues, points of concern, questions and more. We are sorry
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. 

Over the last month and a half, we’ve been reading the discussions about Phasmophobia across so many different channels and taking note of the biggest issues, points of concern, questions and more. We are sorry it’s taken a while to respond, but we wanted to make sure that when we did, we had something of substance for you. Hopefully today, with our updated Roadmap and information on what’s to come to Phasmophobia, we can start to build back your trust and have meaningful conversations around the game we all care so deeply about.

We’ll go in to more detail on everything in this blog, but for those of you that would rather just have the headlines, here we go:

  • The rework of 13 Willow Street will launch July 21st, 2026 alongside a Quality of Life update.

    [/*]

  • A further Quality of Life update will launch in August 2026.

    [/*]

  • Our  upgrade to Unity 6 will take place in October 2026.

    [/*]

  • An additional Player Character update will launch in November, bringing with it improved animations, character models, UI, more clothing and customisation options.

    [/*]

  • Phasmophobia 1.0, as well as our next new map, will now launch in 2027.

    [/*]

A New Roadmap

Over the last month, we’ve taken a look at our plans for 2026 and made important decisions and changes to make sure we get things right. Phasmophobia’s new Roadmap, which can be seen below, focuses on bringing the game back in-line with our vision and one that we hope reignites your passion for it. 

With a focus on quality of life over new content, our various updates will bring with them hundreds of fixes - starting with our first update in July - as well as bringing the game up- to-date with Unity 6. Our second Player Character update will improve and redo areas such as the animations, character models, UI and introduce a host of new customisation options.

Alongside these various updates, you can expect to see more regular Development Previews that’ll take you deeper into what they’ll contain and the improvements they’ll bring to Phasmophobia.

You may notice that a few things have been removed from the Roadmap for 2026, such as Phasmophobia 1.0 and our new map. While we’ll talk more about the 1.0 delay further down in this blog, we want to reassure you that a new map will be coming to Phasmophobia, just a little later than planned.

For now, let’s go into a little detail on the first update - bringing with it the reworked 13 Willow Street. Willow Update

Our 13 Willow Street Rework update will launch on 21st July, 2026, bringing with it the reworked map and the first of our Quality of Life updates.

Before we get into the second part, let’s take a look at the new 13 Willow Street.

As with our rework on 6 Tanglewood Drive, this new version of 13 Willow Street retains an almost identical layout to the location currently in-game but features redesigned rooms, new theming and plenty of new interactions and objects to discover. 13 Willow Street will continue our approach to storytelling in Phasmophobia too, with a story waiting to be pieced together. We can’t wait to hear your theories and thoughts.

Quality of Life Updates Our Quality of Life updates this year will focus on bug fixes, gameplay improvements and the overall stability and playability of the game. Due to the hundreds of bug fixes, changes and new features currently planned for release, we will be releasing two separate Quality of Life updates - the first with the Willow launch and the second in August 2026. 

These updates have been built off the back of the feedback we’ve received since the launch of the Player Character Update, as well as changes we’ve wanted to implement for a long time. We’ll provide full patch notes and details at release of the updates, but here’s a few details on some of the improvements: 

  • New keybinds for pressing 1,2,3 and 4 for the individual inventory slots for keyboard and mouse only.

    [/*]

  • FOV adjustments for held items to increase item visibility.

    [/*]

  • Eye Adaptation option for console players.

    [/*]

  • Moving the Truck closer to map entrances on Prison and Brownstone High School.

    [/*]

  • Restricted map variants for Point Hope, Brownstone High School and Prison.

    [/*]

  • VR improvements; equipment usability changes, belt improvements, character movement and more.

    [/*]

  • The legacy badge screen will be available in the customisation shop to allow you to customise it whenever you want to.

    [/*]

  • The collected bone will show in the Journal.

    [/*]

  • The ghost type can be selected as a custom difficulty option.

    [/*]

  • The zoom feature in the Customisation Shop will now zoom in on the selected area.

    [/*]

  • Ability to raise the Watch while carrying equipment.

    [/*]

  • Improvements to character animations; leg movements, appearance changes when dead.

    [/*]

  • Equipment switching animation speed increases including making opening and closing the journal almost instant.

    [/*]

  • Improvements to photo detection as well as showing an indicator for what you are taking a photo of, similar to video cameras.

    [/*]

  • Equipment can be instantly used, allowing the animation to be skipped.

    [/*]

These two Quality of Life updates will address most of the issues players are encountering, and aim for them to drastically improve the state of Phasmophobia. Other planned updates, such as our move to Unity 6 later in the year, will further improve the stability and performance of the game.

Phasmophobia 1.0 Launches 2027

Earlier this year, we committed to launching Phasmophobia 1.0 at the end of 2026. Now, following a review of our plans and internal processes, we’re making the decision to move the release of 1.0 to the second half of 2027. The game will be coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 at the same time too.

We know this will come as a disappointment to many, but this is the right decision to ensure we deliver the 1.0 version of Phasmophobia that is true to our vision, high quality standards and provides the experience for players that we’ve always wanted to achieve.

We’re not going to stay quiet though. Beginning in 2027 we’ll be bringing you with us during development through regular streams and other means; showcasing our work, sharing some of the secrets of Phasmophobia 1.0 and much more. While we won’t spoil all of the surprises, we want to be as visible as possible for you, hopefully rebuilding the trust that we’ve understandably lost this year.

That said, let’s not wait until the new year to share something.

Below is our first look at the improved model for Patient 07, a fan-favourite who - along with all of our ghost models - is getting a much needed glow up in 1.0.

All of our ghost models will be given a new lease of life(?) with this visual upgrade, but will also be providing their own unique experiences for players. What that entails exactly is something we’ll go into more detail with down the line, but we look forward to sharing that with you when we’re ready to.

Another aspect of 1.0 we want to reveal today is the introduction of Conduits. Replacing the Bone as a collectable during each contract, these are unique items paired with a particular ghost model. 

A Conduit is an item that ties a ghost to our world, preventing them from moving on. These items will provide new lore to the ghost and the overall world of Phasmophobia.

Perhaps you can piece together some tidbits already? 

Rest of the Year

We’ve focused on some of this year’s major updates for Phasmophobia, but as you can see, the Roadmap features more than just these. Following on from our two Quality of Life updates, we’ll be making the jump up to Unity 6 in October. 

This Unity 6 update will provide some crucial stability updates as well as a brand new lighting system for Phasmophobia. We’ll also be implementing further accessibility options such as preferences for colour blind players as well as further quality of life improvements and bug fixes. We’ll dive deeper into these changes in a Development Preview later this year, so please do keep an eye out for that. 

Our second Player Character update will arrive in November, bringing with it brand new customisation options and cosmetics, an improved Shop UI as well as new animations and graphical improvements for our character models in-game, giving each character much more life and personality than is currently available. 

Elsewhere, we’ll see the return of Crimson Eye and Winter’s Jest (sans leaderboards) as well as our third map rework of the year, 42 Edgefield Road. Finally, we’re also planning one extra content update for the game later in 2026 that’ll bring with it changes to our Weekly Challenges ,new Voice Recognition features and more. We will share details on all of our updates when the time is right.

Moving Phasmophobia 1.0 to 2027 allows us the time and resources to get it to the high standard that you all deserve and to rebuild the relationship between Kinetic Games and you, our community. While the news of delays is never a good thing, we do hope that the information we’ve shared with you today helps you understand our decision.

We’re sorry for how Phasmophobia was left following our last update, and we understand your frustration. The Player Character Update did not meet the standards we set for ourselves or the expectations you have for the game. Our next update is the first step in a new direction, and we look forward to sharing it with you on July 21st.

Thank you, Ghost Hunters, for your time reading this blog, your passion and support for Phasmophobia and so much more.

items.title: You Aren’t Prepared For How Scary Phasmophobia 1.0 Is Meant To Be
Phasmophobia 1.0 Aims for Unprecedented Immersion and Fear
items.summary: Phasmophobia's 1.0 update aims to terrify us with new ghosts, audio improvements, and more.
items.content:
Phasmophobia was always a bit of a dream game for me, as someone too meek to actually go out and search for ghosts, and much more content staying in the comfort of my own, non-haunted home. Needless to say, I put hundreds of hours into Phasmophobia when it first released in Early Access back in 2020. After what seems like a lifetime, it's finally nearing its highly anticipated 1.0 launch, promising to up the scares to a whole new level.

To that end, we attended a hands-off preview session at Summer Game Fest 2026, which first went over the details on a rework for Willow Street -- an iconic location from the game -- and finally what we can expect to see for the 1.0 release, slated for 2027.

Willow Street - Cat Trees and Horrifying Paintings So, it's been a hot minute since I've played Phasmophobia . My usual group fell apart, and this is certainly a game that benefits from having a party of friends to play with. As I understand it, several locations from the base game were reworked, and Willow Street is next on the list.

The original Willow Street, along with other locations in Phasmophobia, used pre-made, purchased assets. That was when developer Kinetic Games was a one-man show, but now it has dozens of developers at its disposal. These reworks are hand-made with new assets while also retaining the "spirit" of the original location, no pun intended.

I always enjoyed the residential houses in Phasmophobia more than any other location, so to see Willow Street getting a rework is great. This rework has a notable Palm Springs vibe in the outdoors portion, and upon entering the house, we see that it's a very lived-in building with a story to tell.

Cat trees. They're everywhere. On the walls, around the room. Whoever lives here really loves cats. I never really thought much about the stories of locations when investigating them, but with so much more detail and character, it brings Willow Street to life.

By and large, the floor plan is the same, though the rooms themselves are different. The cat-oriented room, for instance, but also the kitchen with its island counter. Going into the garage, it's a full-on gym with equipment like weights and a treadmill, while the former storage room is now a shower.

Going into the basement, it has a distinct man cave vibe, with a big TV, a beautiful sectional, and even a full-service bar. There are some hiding places scattered around to avoid ghosts, but with Willow Street being a smaller house in general, tense counters are almost a certainty.

The most terrifying room of the house is no doubt the atelier, formerly the boys' bedroom. It is filled with sinister, dark paintings, showing that the artist is clearly a disturbed individual. And this seems to be the general vibe Kinetic Games is going for with their reworks and the future of Phasmophobia. They want locations to tell a story with bits and details, but they also want players to interpret it for themselves and unravel the mysteries behind the hauntings.

1.0 Aims for a AAA Audio Experience Speaking of the future, 1.0 is on the horizon. This is where Phasmophobia' s Summer Game Fest presentation really impressed me. We take a surprisingly deep, sophisticated look at some new gameplay features and enhancements that will definitely add to the horror element of the game. Namely, audio.

Sound was a large focus here, and CEO and Game Director Daniel Knight assures me that they are aiming for AAA-quality sound design. This is achieved by completely revamping and replacing all of the pre-existing audio in Phasmophoia. Kinetic Games aims for dynamic sound that reacts to the state of the game, spatial audio, and more.

To highlight a few impressive details, Knight mentions "in-depth randomization," which is to ensure that no two sounds are exactly the same, just like real life. Sound will also now react differently depending on the material properties of the room. The wooden nature of the rustic Bleasdale Farmhouse will be distinctly different from the cold interiors of the Prison.

Vocal communication is also a key element to Phasmophobia , so when you're talking with a friend, it's going to sound as realistic as possible. The voice chat is now processed by acoustics, so the screams of your wimpiest buddy will now reverberate around a location.

Redesigned, Creepier Ghosts 1.0 also promises to bring a redesign for Phasmophobia 's iconic ghosts. While we've grown to love (or loathe) these phantoms, they are receiving redesigns to fit more cohesively with the game's narrative updates. The ghosts will be more distinct and are more or less their own characters, now.

items.title: Summer Game Fest 2026 Hands-On – Phasmophobia
items.content:
Phasmophobia entered our lives in 2020 and quickly became a huge sensation, being a favorite for streamers on Twitch and YouTube. With its inventiveness with features such as speech recognition, allowing for the ghosts to understand key words spoken by players and alter the experience accordingly, it proved to be an engaging and tension-filled experience. Now, six years later, Phasmophobia is reworking the 13 Willow Street map, following the success of the updated Grafton and Bleasdale farmhouse, as well as 6 Tanglewood Drive.

What else do you need in a man cave aside from a couch, a TV, and ghost or two?

The new version of the 13 Willow Street map will include improvements such as room redesigns, altered hiding spots, and brand-new lore, which will embedded within the game. This will be in addition to the inclusion of the first of two major quality-of-life updates planned for the game, which will improve player character models, animations, and customization options. Fan favorite locations such as Crimson Eye, Winter’s Jest, and Edgefield will also make a triumphant return later this year.

Now while I wasn’t able to play the demo for myself, I was treated to a guided play session by one of the developers. This demo was intended to showcase the dev’s vision for the game’s full 1.0 launch coming up in 2027, using the Unity 6 engine. What I saw got me very excited as the graphics are shaping up to be vastly improved from the original version. They’ve updated the decorations in the rooms to reflect current trends, such as a garage that has been converted into a weight room (something I’ve seen plenty of in real life), as well as transforming the basement into a game room/man cave. The lighting is much more dramatic, and the level of detail within the objects within the room is impressive.

The level of detail in each new ghost design is remarkable.

They’re also completing overhauling the sound design, giving a far more realistic and immersive experience. For example, it was raining in-game during the play session, and you could hear the difference in the sound the rain made from hitting the roof versus the window. The devs are also playing around with how the acoustics within Phasmophobia work. This means that not only will voices become clearer or more muffled depending on where each player is within the location, but they will also alter depending on which room they’re in. The dining room with its open spaces and hardwood floors will give voices more resonance and echo, while the walled in, carpeted basement will sound more dampened.

Meet some of the new spirits who are going to haunt your dreams.

This is all incredibly exciting, but I’m personally most looking forward to are the new ghost designs and lore. There are six new ghost designs in Phasmophobia , which will replace the current models. These include spirits such as a drowned woman, and electrocuted woman, a mental patient, and a child (because of course there has to be a creepy kid). The designs also include distinctive animations, such as the drowned woman having floating hair when you encounter her and the mental patient whipping the straightjacket ties. They’ll also spawn in areas that reflect how they died, like the drowned woman spawning near water and the burn victim appearing near fire.

Each ghost has its own corresponding trinket, something that anchors them to each location. These include things such as a broken lightbulb, a smashed watch, and a child’s bracelet. Players will need to find these items in order to get more of each spirit’s back story and potentially allow them to finally move on. This is my favorite aspect of the upcoming changes as it gives an already fun game even more depth.

Each of these seemingly mundane trinkets is a conduit for one of the spirits.

The new 13 Willow Street map will be available on July on July 21st for all platforms, and will be a free upgrade for those who already have the game. Phasmophobia version 1.0 is set to release next year, giving me more than enough reason to revisit this game with some friends.

items.title: Phasmophobia - Hotfix v0.17.1.5
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we have released a hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing two issues raised by the community this week. The update is now available across all platforms. Patch notes: Fixed an issue that caused players to bec
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we have released a hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing two issues raised by the community this week. The update is now available across all platforms.

Patch notes:

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to become softlocked when using the Spirit Box while using a controller.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue for Steam players that caused Voice Recognition to not work.

    [/*]

  • If you are encountering issues with Voice Recognition on the "Voice" setting on PC, please ensure that the default microphone assigned on your PC is the microphone specified in your windows settings.

    [/*]

items.title: Phasmophobia v0.17.1.4 - Patch Notes
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we have released our latest update for Phasmophobia, addressing several known issues in the game. We are aware of the current ongoing issues in-game and are currently implementing fixes and improvements in
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we have released our latest update for Phasmophobia, addressing several known issues in the game.

We are aware of the current ongoing issues in-game and are currently implementing fixes and improvements in future updates for Phasmophobia. We’ll be sharing our plans for the rest of 2026 later this month with you all, including a timeline for fixes and updates related to both the Player Character Update and game in general. In the meantime, please continue to provide feedback and bug reports so that our team can investigate as much as possible. You can submit any and all issues via our Support Centre.

New

  • The VR belt has been reimplemented following player feedback. Players will now also be able to switch between having their Journal and Walkie Talkie on the belt or their shoulder. 

    • The VR belt will only be visible locally, players who are looking at a VR player will see the equipment on their body instead.

      [/*]
    [/*]

Changes

  • The line of sight ghost speed accelerations implemented in the Player Character Update have been reverted to their original state.

    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to become softlocked when using the Spirit Box before interacting with the Truck’s CCTV.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to kill players through floors.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to kill players outside.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 3 Firelight to not work correctly if placed while lit.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where player jackets would appear incorrectly when loading into a Multiplayer lobby.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where pressing ‘Join’ in Multiplayer without having a code entered would cause issues creating lobbies, and remove audio from the game.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where VR players were unable to use Text Input for the Spirit Box, Monkey Paw and Ouija Board.

    [/*]

Known Issues

  • Players across all platforms are experiencing issues with voice recognition/voice chat. A fix is being investigated.

    [/*]

Please note: Following this update, players on Windows 7/8 or other out-of-date Windows installs may need to run Windows Update (and restart) for voice chat to connect. This is due to a recent security certificate update on our voice servers. More info can be found here.

As you continue your investigations in Phasmophobia, if you encounter bugs, issues or simply have feedback for our team, please use our Support Centre to submit your reports.
